page: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features an x86_64 architecture with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the server includes 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and PassMark CPU encryption tests placing in the top 10% of all tested configurations. The hardware supports advanced vector extensions, including AVX-512, which contributes to strong performance in specialized tasks such as ray tracing, image processing, and software compilation. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with read-only large block speeds reaching 335.25 GB/sec. Single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6id.metal offers high resource density by combining dedicated bare-metal compute resources with a substantial local NVMe storage allocation. This configuration eliminates virtualization overhead and provides high-speed local data access, making it highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads that require fast temporary storage. Appropriate workloads include high-performance computing, large-scale database operations, data compilation, and multi-threaded batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server designed for resource-intensive workloads. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs, 64 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. The hardware is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a substantial 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, encryption capabilities, and image processing speeds, alongside strong memory bandwidth. While single-core performance remains average, the dedicated bare-metal architecture and integrated high-speed local storage deliver excellent qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-density compute and rapid local data access. This server is highly suited for high-performance computing, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded batch processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for compute-intensive and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server combining dedicated 128-vCPU compute power with massive local NVMe SSD storage for intensive multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
256.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption benchmark performance
Best suited for high-performance computing, databases, and compilation
